fr:tree.fr:mainmatter:
We know that the optimal codeword for P has lengths approximately equal to \ell _{P}(x)\approx \log (1/p(x)) , where p denotes the pmf of P . Whereas the optimal codeword for Q assigns lengths \ell _{Q}(x)\approx \log (1/q(x)) . Then, the redundancy is
\begin {align*} \mathcal {R}(Q,P)&=\mathbb {E}_{X\sim Q}[\ell _{P}(X)-\ell _{Q}(X)]\approx \mathbb {E}_{X\sim Q}[-\log (p(X))+\log (q(X))] \\ &=\mathbb {E}_{X\sim Q}\left [\log \left (\frac {q(X)}{p(X)}\right )\right ]=D_{\mathrm {KL}}(Q\parallel P) .\end {align*}

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Suppose X\sim Q , but we encode it using an optimal code for P . Then, the redundancy incurred is approximately equal to D_{KL}(Q\parallel P) .

fr:tree.fr:mainmatter:
We know that the optimal codeword for P has lengths approximately equal to \ell _{P}(x)\approx \log (1/p(x)) , where p denotes the pmf of P . Whereas the optimal codeword for Q assigns lengths \ell _{Q}(x)\approx \log (1/q(x)) . Then, the redundancy is
\begin {align*} \mathcal {R}(Q,P)&=\mathbb {E}_{X\sim Q}[\ell _{P}(X)-\ell _{Q}(X)]\approx \mathbb {E}_{X\sim Q}[-\log (p(X))+\log (q(X))] \\ &=\mathbb {E}_{X\sim Q}\left [\log \left (\frac {q(X)}{p(X)}\right )\right ]=D_{\mathrm {KL}}(Q\parallel P) .\end {align*}
